findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised span at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:364-390 shows at least one condition that triggers pushback or renegotiation before assent and includes embodied consequence for both characters; FAIL if the exchange remains a sequential "term -> Done" checklist.
findings.problem: Sanitization drift at the emotional apex: after Ravi names coercive motive, the scene collapses into a clean condition-checklist ("Done" loop) that summarizes conflict instead of rendering immediate, messy consequence in-body.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rewrite the confrontation turn at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:364-390 so each condition is negotiated through behavior and resistance (interruptions, failed wording, physical reactions, memory intrusions) rather than rapid procedural assent; keep boundaries, but make agreement costly on-page.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if, in snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:354-382, each reply adds new leverage (concession, refusal, or specific consequence) and no identical one-word acknowledgment appears more than once; FAIL if repeated placeholder replies still carry the scene.
findings.problem: Transcript-cadence compression flattens leverage shifts: repeated low-information replies ("I know", "Done") reduce pressure exchange and blur idiolect distinction at a high-stakes moment.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:354-382 so Ravi answers with register-specific language (procedural avoidance cracking into fear) and Anika counters with concrete boundary language tied to lived cost, not repeated minimal acknowledgments.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if those cited beats are rewritten with distinct physical actions and the smile/nod defaults occur no more than twice total across the revised chapter; FAIL if the same default gestures still dominate those moments.
findings.problem: Recurring gesture formulas (almost/half-smile and repeated nod beats) appear more than twice and start reading as autopilot emotional shorthand instead of scene-specific behavior.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:41, :231, :428 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:165, :364, :400, replace default smile/nod cues with context-bound actions that expose distinct states (fatigue, avoidance, guarded concession, grief control).

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:392-396 contains no meta disclaimer about melodrama and includes at least two concrete physical cues of emotional strain; FAIL if the narration still instructs the reader how to grade the emotion.
findings.problem: Timidity at the vulnerability peak: the narration self-protects with a meta qualifier ("not crying in any dramatic movie way") that distances the moment instead of staying inside immediate emotional/physical reality.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rewrite snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:392-396 to remove anti-melodrama framing and render Ravi's grief state through concrete sensory/body detail and Anika's in-the-moment perception.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if, in revised snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:278-306, at least two dialogue or action beats explicitly register unresolved debt/trust tension before line-level transition into the ask at line 310 equivalent; FAIL if the section functions as detached comic interlude.
findings.problem: Causal pressure slackens after the arrears reveal: extended anecdotal run delays consequence-bearing interaction, so the chapter's central conflict temporarily loses propulsion.
findings.rewrite_direction: Tighten and retune snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:278-306 so neighborhood/school anecdotes still play, but each beat carries unresolved debt/trust pressure (through subtext, interruption, or failed topic-shift) and points directly to the later confrontation.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised cited spans preserve Ravi's avoidance voice while limiting explicit meta-keywords ("logistics"/"spreadsheet") to at most one total use and replacing the others with concrete, in-scene phrasing; FAIL if repeated keyword signaling remains the primary delivery mechanism.
findings.problem: Mechanical reuse of stress-signature keywords ("logistics", spreadsheet/control framing) becomes overt labeling in dialogue, reducing lived speech texture and making subtext feel pre-annotated.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise confrontation language at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:183-201 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:340-350 so motive is conveyed through situation-specific diction and concrete references, not repeated meta-vocabulary.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: Lean into uncomfortable specificity when it changes behavior: pain detail, debt arithmetic, manipulative care, and mixed motives. In edge cases, prefer the truest in-character action over protective narration, then carry the real consequence forward instead of immediately moralizing it away.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If coercion, grief, injury, or economic shame appears, language must stay specific and embodied; vague softening triggers mandatory rewrite.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High - contractions are the spoken default; uncontracted forms are reserved for formal authority, deliberate emphasis, or emotional freezing.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Vary scene count and dialogue density chapter to chapter: rotate match compression, slow inventory aftermath, institutional pressure scenes, and quiet domestic intervals so adjacent chapters do not share the same structural pulse.
prose_style_profile.diction: Concrete contemporary diction with class-aware specificity; avoid abstract therapy language unless a character would actually use it.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third anchored in Anika for most chapters, with brief controlled slips toward Ravi in designated confrontation and aftermath chapters.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Alternate clipped athletic sequences with longer reflective cadences; sentence length should track exertion, shame, and release.
